Wattle Creek Holdings Pty Ltd (Australia) — Privacy & Retention Workflow and MNPI Controls (Privacy Act 1988 (Cth), Corporations Act, ASX/ASIC)
This note applies to employment, HSE, payroll, credit assessment, vendor onboarding, and investor relations conducted in Australia.
Personal information means information or opinion about an identified or reasonably identifiable individual collected and handled in accordance with the Australian Privacy Principles.
Collection points include paper and online forms with labels such as Full Name, Residential Address, AU Passport No, TFN (optional), BSB/Account, Contact Number, and Email.
Formatting-only examples (invalid/placeholder): TFN 123 456 782; ACN 000 000 000; BSB 000-000; Account 12345678X; AU Passport <QZ1234567>; Street <0 Example Rd, Nulltown NSW 2999>.
Privacy contact for requests and complaints: privacy.office@wattlecreek.example.au; general enquiries helpline 1300 000 111 (public-facing line; do not use for emergencies).
Registered office for correspondence: Suite 4, 1 Paperbark Place, Nulltown NSW 2999; do not send identity documents by post unless requested.
Use and retention: TFNs are used solely for tax withholding and are deleted after first PAYG processing; payroll summaries and credit decision records are retained for 7 years, then securely destroyed.
Health and safety incident data is stored separately with restricted access and reviewed for deletion at 2-year intervals unless required for legal claims.
MNPI handling: information not generally available that would reasonably be expected to materially affect Wattle Creek financial products is classified as MNPI under the Corporations Act and internal policy aligned to ASIC guidance.
Examples of restricted items include draft financials, unannounced executive changes, and confidential term sheets; ASX-released announcements and already public summaries are not treated as MNPI.
Access controls: AU-hosted systems, least-privilege roles, logging, and quarterly disposal runs; automated redaction applied to attachments containing TFNs or passport fields.
Disclosures: service providers (payroll, HSE systems, credit reporting agencies) are engaged under contract; no overseas disclosure occurs without due diligence and contractual safeguards consistent with APP 8.
Exercising access/correction rights: submit a DSAR by email with subject “DSAR — AU”, provide minimal verification details (e.g., last payslip ID only), and we will respond within a reasonable period.
